deck.title: RawrXD — Inference Router & Terminal Parity
deck.subtitle: Competitive framing · Ollama / vLLM positioning · April 2026
deck.footnote: Chart numbers are illustrative for narrative decks; benchmark on your own fleet before procurement.

positioning.intro: Ollama optimizes for single-node developer experience. vLLM optimizes for batched GPU serving. RawrXD targets IDE-embedded agent loops, native Win32 shell fidelity, and toolchain-adjacent inference—not a drop-in replacement for either, but the control plane that unifies them inside the editor.

rows.label: Primary fit
values: IDE-native agents, terminal mirroring, GGUF + bridge

rows.label: Operational surface
values: Win32 UI + optional local servers; agent tool plane

rows.label: Differentiator
values: Cursor/VS Code terminal parity + read-only agent pane

timeSeries.title: Agent tool round-trip p95 (ms) — sprint trend
